text.title: VUI Market Context Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when agents assemble VUI market briefs; each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Agent returns plausible-looking adoption figures with fake or unresolvable source URLs.
antipattern.root-cause: LLMs synthesise numbers that pattern-match training data and confabulate URLs without verifying them.
antipattern.fix: Require source_url for every data row. Reject rows where the URL host is outside the trusted-source allowlist; HEAD-check every URL during validation.

antipattern.symptom: Brief claims "62% adoption" combining smart-speaker household share with voice-assistant user share as if they measured the same thing.
antipattern.root-cause: Different sources publish in different denominators; synthesis pipelines collapse them.
antipattern.fix: Enforce the `denominator` enum (smart-speaker-households | voice-assistant-users | voice-search-query-share) on every row; the validator refuses cross-denominator arithmetic.

antipattern.symptom: Brief presents a US-only Pew figure as global; platform comparison weighted on wrong geography produces a wrong-platform recommendation.
antipattern.root-cause: Geo field stripped during summarisation; downstream consumer assumes worldwide.
antipattern.fix: `geo` is required per data row; platform comparison table MUST tabulate per target geo (US, EU, APAC) before recommendation.

antipattern.symptom: Brief over a year old reused for a 2026 platform decision; the data predates LLM-native voice category and recommends Bixby for a non-Korean market.
antipattern.root-cause: Briefs lack freshness gating; consumers reuse without checking refreshed_at.
antipattern.fix: Validator refuses publish when today − refreshed_at > 90 days; refresh-script.py is the canonical refresh entry point.
